LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS POLK STREET PARTNERS, LP, JEFFREY LITKE AND STELLA CHU'S MOTION TO STRIKE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT, AS TO PUNITIVE DAMAGES ALLEGATIONS IN THE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS GRANTED. EVEN IF THE RAYBURNS PROVED ALL OF THEIR FACTUAL (AS DISTINCT FROM LEGAL) ALLEGATIONS REGARDING PUNITIVE DAMAGES, THEY WOULD NOT BE ENTITLED TO PUNITIVE DAMAGES SINCE THOSE FACTUAL ALLEGATIONS DO NOT SHOW THAT THE DEFENDANTS ACTED DESPICABLY. WRITTEN ORDER IS AMENDED BY INTERLINEATIONS, SIGNED AND FILED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: JOSE RIOS MERIDA; REPORTER: CAROL KAREN, CSR NO. 8189, EMAIL: CKAREN@SFTC.ORG. (D302)

Notice Of Motion To Compel And For Sanctions
Off Calendar Jul-19-2017 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances in the matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Ruling: PLAINTIFF STEPHEN RAYBURN, MARIAN RAYBURN Motion To Compel And For Sanctions is taken off calendar per the request of the moving party. Judge Pro Tem: Thomas Cohen; Clerk: Felicia Green; Not reported; Dept. 302.

Notice Of Motion For Leave To Amend Cross-Complaint
Off Calendar Jun-15-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANTS/CROSS-COMPLAINANTS POLK STREET PARTNERS LP and JEFFREY LITKE'S Motion For Leave To Amend Cross-Complaint is GRANTED. No opposition filed. Order is signed in Open Court. Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR 711, ckaren@sftc.org =(302/hk)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Apr-12-2017 Set For Trial On Jan-02-18 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

OBJECTION HEARING CALENDAR
Off Calendar Apr-27-2017 Off Calendar

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Feb-27-2017 Continued To Apr-26-17 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Feb-14-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, Samuel P. Cheadle of the law offices of Chavez & Gertler LLP (415.381.5599) appears as counsel for plaintiff/defendant/moving/opposing party

Attorney, Edward J. Rodzewich of the law offices of Stratman Patterson & Hunter (510.457.3460) appears as counsel for defendant/opposing party

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides as set forth in the transcript of the certified shorthand reporter. The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

DEFENDANTS POLK STREET PARTNERS, LP, JEFFREY LITKE and STELLA CHU'S MOTION TO STRIKE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT, as to punitive damages allegations in the first amended complaint is GRANTED. Even if the Rayburns proved all of their factual (as distinct from legal) allegations regarding punitive damages, they would not be entitled to punitive damages since those factual allegations do not show that the defendants acted despicably. Written order is amended by interlineations, signed and filed in open court.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Jose Rios Merida; Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR No. 8189, email: ckaren@sftc.org. (D302)
